comment_text.sn3.19:2.2: This accords with Arthaśāstra 3.5.28, “A king should take property for which there is no heir”.
comment_text.sn3.19:2.7: Hemp (sāṇa) made a rough  cloth. It was not known as an intoxicant.
comment_text.sn3.19:2.9: Compare the splendid chariot of the brahmin Jānussoṇi (SN 45.4:1.3).
comment_text.sn3.19:3.2: The proper use of wealth includes enjoying it oneself as well as helping others.
comment_text.sn3.19:8.3: Nisabha means “bull” and “chief”, both of which are covered by “boss”.

root_text.sn3.19:1.2: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo divā divassa yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn3.19:1.3: “handa kuto nu tvaṁ, mahārāja, āgacchasi divā divassā”ti?
root_text.sn3.19:2.2: Tamahaṁ aputtakaṁ sāpateyyaṁ rājantepuraṁ atiharitvā āgacchāmi.
root_text.sn3.19:2.3: Asīti, bhante, satasahassāni hiraññasseva, ko pana vādo rūpiyassa.
root_text.sn3.19:2.4: Tassa kho pana, bhante, seṭṭhissa gahapatissa evarūpo bhattabhogo ahosi—
root_text.sn3.19:3.2: Asappuriso kho, mahārāja, uḷāre bhoge labhitvā nevattānaṁ sukheti pīṇeti, na mātāpitaro sukheti pīṇeti, na puttadāraṁ sukheti pīṇeti, na dāsakammakaraporise sukheti pīṇeti, na mittāmacce sukheti pīṇeti, na samaṇabrāhmaṇesu uddhaggikaṁ dakkhiṇaṁ patiṭṭhāpeti sovaggikaṁ sukhavipākaṁ saggasaṁvattanikaṁ.
root_text.sn3.19:3.3: Tassa te bhoge evaṁ sammā aparibhuñjiyamāne rājāno vā haranti corā vā haranti aggi vā ḍahati udakaṁ vā vahati appiyā vā dāyādā haranti.
root_text.sn3.19:3.4: Evaṁsa te, mahārāja, bhogā sammā aparibhuñjiyamānā parikkhayaṁ gacchanti, no paribhogaṁ.
root_text.sn3.19:4.1: Seyyathāpi, mahārāja, amanussaṭṭhāne pokkharaṇī acchodakā sītodakā sātodakā setodakā supatitthā ramaṇīyā.
root_text.sn3.19:4.2: Taṁ jano neva hareyya na piveyya na nahāyeyya na yathāpaccayaṁ vā kareyya.
root_text.sn3.19:4.3: Evañhi taṁ, mahārāja, udakaṁ sammā aparibhuñjiyamānaṁ parikkhayaṁ gaccheyya, no paribhogaṁ.
root_text.sn3.19:4.4: Evameva kho, mahārāja, asappuriso uḷāre bhoge labhitvā nevattānaṁ sukheti pīṇeti, na mātāpitaro sukheti pīṇeti, na puttadāraṁ sukheti pīṇeti, na dāsakammakaraporise sukheti pīṇeti, na mittāmacce sukheti pīṇeti, na samaṇabrāhmaṇesu uddhaggikaṁ dakkhiṇaṁ patiṭṭhāpeti sovaggikaṁ sukhavipākaṁ saggasaṁvattanikaṁ.
root_text.sn3.19:4.5: Tassa te bhoge evaṁ sammā aparibhuñjiyamāne rājāno vā haranti, corā vā haranti, aggi vā ḍahati, udakaṁ vā vahati, appiyā vā dāyādā haranti.
root_text.sn3.19:4.6: Evaṁsa te, mahārāja, bhogā sammā aparibhuñjiyamānā parikkhayaṁ gacchanti, no paribhogaṁ.
root_text.sn3.19:5.1: Sappuriso ca kho, mahārāja, uḷāre bhoge labhitvā attānaṁ sukheti pīṇeti, mātāpitaro sukheti pīṇeti, puttadāraṁ sukheti pīṇeti, dāsakammakaraporise sukheti pīṇeti, mittāmacce sukheti pīṇeti, samaṇabrāhmaṇesu uddhaggikaṁ dakkhiṇaṁ patiṭṭhāpeti sovaggikaṁ sukhavipākaṁ saggasaṁvattanikaṁ.
root_text.sn3.19:5.2: Tassa te bhoge evaṁ sammā paribhuñjiyamāne neva rājāno haranti, na corā haranti, na aggi ḍahati, na udakaṁ vahati, na appiyā dāyādā haranti.
root_text.sn3.19:5.3: Evaṁsa te, mahārāja, bhogā sammā paribhuñjiyamānā paribhogaṁ gacchanti, no parikkhayaṁ.
root_text.sn3.19:6.1: Seyyathāpi, mahārāja, gāmassa vā nigamassa vā avidūre pokkharaṇī acchodakā sītodakā sātodakā setodakā supatitthā ramaṇīyā.
root_text.sn3.19:6.2: Tañca udakaṁ jano hareyyapi piveyyapi nahāyeyyapi yathāpaccayampi kareyya.
root_text.sn3.19:6.3: Evañhi taṁ, mahārāja, udakaṁ sammā paribhuñjiyamānaṁ paribhogaṁ gaccheyya, no parikkhayaṁ.
root_text.sn3.19:6.4: Evameva kho, mahārāja, sappuriso uḷāre bhoge labhitvā attānaṁ sukheti pīṇeti, mātāpitaro sukheti pīṇeti, puttadāraṁ sukheti pīṇeti, dāsakammakaraporise sukheti pīṇeti, mittāmacce sukheti pīṇeti, samaṇabrāhmaṇesu uddhaggikaṁ dakkhiṇaṁ patiṭṭhāpeti sovaggikaṁ sukhavipākaṁ saggasaṁvattanikaṁ.
root_text.sn3.19:6.5: Tassa te bhoge evaṁ sammā paribhuñjiyamāne neva rājāno haranti, na corā haranti, na aggi ḍahati, na udakaṁ vahati, na appiyā dāyādā haranti.
root_text.sn3.19:6.6: Evaṁsa te, mahārāja, bhogā sammā paribhuñjiyamānā paribhogaṁ gacchanti, no parikkhayan”ti.

translation_text.sn3.19:1.2: Then King Pasenadi of Kosala went up to the Buddha in the middle of the day, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him,
translation_text.sn3.19:1.3: “So, great king, where are you coming from in the middle of the day?”
translation_text.sn3.19:2.1: “Sir, here in Sāvatthī a financier householder has passed away.
translation_text.sn3.19:2.2: Since he died childless, I have come after transferring his fortune to the royal compound.
translation_text.sn3.19:2.3: There was eight million in gold coin, not to mention the silver coin.
translation_text.sn3.19:2.4: And yet that financier ate meals of
translation_text.sn3.19:2.5: rough gruel with false black pepper.
translation_text.sn3.19:2.8: He traveled around in a vehicle that was
translation_text.sn3.19:2.9: a dilapidated little cart, holding a leaf as parasol.”
translation_text.sn3.19:3.1: “That’s so true, great king! That’s so true!
translation_text.sn3.19:3.2: When an untrue person has acquired notable wealth they don’t make themselves happy and pleased. Nor do they make their mother and father, partners and children, bondservants, workers, and staff, and friends and colleagues happy and pleased. And they don’t establish an uplifting religious donation for ascetics and brahmins that’s conducive to heaven, ripens in happiness, and leads to heaven.
translation_text.sn3.19:3.3: Because they haven’t made proper use of that wealth, rulers or bandits take it, or fire consumes it, or flood sweeps it away, or unloved heirs take it.
translation_text.sn3.19:3.4: Since that wealth is not properly utilized, it’s wasted, not used.
translation_text.sn3.19:4.1: Suppose there was a lotus pond in an uninhabited region with clear, sweet, cool water, clean, with smooth banks, delightful.
translation_text.sn3.19:4.2: But people don’t collect it or drink it or bathe in it or use it for any purpose.
translation_text.sn3.19:4.3: Since that water is not properly utilized, it’s wasted, not used.
translation_text.sn3.19:4.4: In the same way, when an untrue person has acquired notable wealth …
translation_text.sn3.19:5.1: When a true person has acquired notable wealth they make themselves happy and pleased. And they make their mother and father, partners and children, bondservants, workers, and staff, and friends and colleagues happy and pleased. And they establish an uplifting religious donation for ascetics and brahmins that’s conducive to heaven, ripens in happiness, and leads to heaven.
translation_text.sn3.19:5.2: Because they make proper use of that wealth, rulers or bandits don’t take it, fire doesn’t consume it, flood doesn’t sweep it away, and unloved heirs don’t take it.
translation_text.sn3.19:5.3: Since that wealth is properly utilized, it’s used, not wasted.
translation_text.sn3.19:6.1: Suppose there was a lotus pond not far from a town or village with clear, sweet, cool water, clean, with smooth banks, delightful.
translation_text.sn3.19:6.2: And people collected it and drank it and bathed in it and used it for their own purpose.
translation_text.sn3.19:6.3: Since that water is properly utilized, it’s used, not wasted.
translation_text.sn3.19:6.4: In the same way, when a true person has acquired notable wealth …
translation_text.sn3.19:7.1: As cool water in an uninhabited region
translation_text.sn3.19:7.3: so too when a reprobate acquires wealth,
translation_text.sn3.19:7.4: they neither use it themselves nor give it away.
translation_text.sn3.19:8.1: When the attentive and sensible acquire wealth,
translation_text.sn3.19:8.3: That boss, having supported the family unit,
translation_text.sn3.19:8.4: blameless, goes to a heavenly place.”

reference_text.sn3.19:5.1: ms12S1_702, ndp12.90, pts-vp-pli1ed1.91, pts-vp-pli2ed1.204, sya15.132, vri23.109
